Article Title: 167_Optimizing HMI for Intelligent Electric Vehicles Using BCI and Deep Neural Networks with Genetic Algorithms
Design Discipline: This research explores the interaction design of the main control human-machine interaction interfaces (HMIs) for intelligent electric vehicles (EVs) by integrating neural network predictions with genetic algorithm optimizations. This study analyzed primary HMIs for intelligent electric vehicles (EVs) using established guidelines, industry standards, and the Electric Vehicle UI Design Specification. The 15 items were categorized into five factors: Factor 1: Layout mode, Factor 2: Interactive mode, Factor 3: Color mode, Factor 4: Feedback mode, Factor 5: Prompt model.
Data About: The data collection procedure involved participants wearing the Mindwave EEG device and starting the data collection app on a PC. The collected data included EEG raw data and five sub-frequency types: Delta, Theta, Alpha, Beta, Gamma, and e-Sense. Real-time EEG data acquisition can accurately monitor the driver's cognitive state during operation.
Data Modality: The data collection procedure involved participants wearing the Mindwave EEG device and starting the data collection app on a PC. The collected data included EEG raw data and five sub-frequency types: Delta, Theta, Alpha, Beta, Gamma, and e-Sense. The experimental setup defined specific parameters for EEG data collection: a sampling rate of 512 Hz to ensure high-resolution data capture. The headset displayed the virtual driving environment, overlaying the AR HMI onto the physical control screens of the EV.
AI methods: This study utilizes a brain—computer interface (BCI)—based deep neural network (DNN) and genetic algorithm (GA) method. The deep neural network (DNN) topology linking HMI visual coding to e-Sense prediction comprises three layers: an input layer with 26 neurons, three fully connected hidden layers, and an output layer with one neurone. The network utilized rectified linear unit (ReLU) activation functions across the hidden layers, with a regression layer at the output. Deep neural networks (DNNs) play a key role in the personalized human-machine interaction interfaces (HMIs) of intelligent electric vehicles. By analyzing complex user data, interactions are customized, the learning environment is optimized, and the user experience is enhanced.
AI Assistance Types (Lee and Kim, 2025): The DNN interpreted EEG data to provide insights into cognitive and attention states, which were used as inputs for the GA. The GA applied evolutionary processes to a population of HMI designs, with fitness evaluated based on DNN predictions. This iterative process identified the most effective HMI configurations.
Design Phase: This iterative process identified the most effective HMI configurations. The GA applied evolutionary processes to a population of HMI designs, with fitness evaluated based on DNN predictions. After 185 iterations, the optimal individual was identified with a preference score of approximately 5.570. The simulation involved driving on a straight, two-lane city road for 5 min at a speed of 30 km/h. Participants included 32 university students (18 males, 14 females) aged 19 to 25, all specializing in product design.
Design Practice/Task: This research identified essential HMI elements for intelligent electric vehicles to understand user interaction needs. The developed DNN-GA analyzed user behavior patterns from EEG data to predict preferences and attention levels during driving. These predictions guided the GA in optimizing HMI layouts. In this experiment, EEG data were measured using an AR system integrated into a real vehicle cockpit environment, providing an immersive and realistic driving experience. Participants sequentially interacted with the AR HMI prototype's main control interface, performing tasks such as adjusting the rearview mirror, turning on driving lights, adjusting the seat, closing the sunshade, activating the 360° driving camera, turning on music, and, finally, opening the map. This setup ensured a realistic and interactive evaluation of the HMI design.

Article Title: 108_Exploiting Generative Models for Performance Predictions of 3D Car Designs
Design Discipline: In automotive digital development, engineers utilize multiple virtual prototyping tools to design and assess the performance of 3D shapes. Computer-aided design (CAD) and engineering (CAE) tools accelerate various tasks in the automotive development process, particularly through virtual prototyping.
Data About: In automotive digital development, engineers utilize multiplevirtualprototypingtoolstodesignandassesstheperformanceof3Dshapes. We envision a 3D design assistance system for design exploration with performance assessment in the automotive domain. We evaluate the accuracy of the multi-output surrogate model for predicting performance metrics of 3D car designs from the learned latent representations.
Data Modality: 3D point clouds which are a list of data point coordinates in 3D space are sampled directly from the surface of the CAE models, require less pre-processing effort, and preserve enough geometric details. We sample 3500 shapes from the car class and each shape consists of 24578 points.
AI methods: Geometric deep-generative models, like autoencoders (AEs)[1] and variational autoencoders (VAEs) [2] learn low-dimensional latent representations of 3D geometric models in an unsupervised fashion, which allows to efficiently explore the learned design features and generate novel realistic 3D designs [3], [4]. Furthermore, the latent space of autoencoders has also been utilized for many predictive tasks in the chemistry and biology domain[5], allowing the assessment of the chemical compounds before committing to an expensive synthesis.
AI Assistance Types (Lee and Kim, 2025): we use machine learning-based surrogate models to predict the performances of car shapes based on the low-dimensional representation learned by 3D point cloud (variational) autoencoders. Furthermore, we exploit the stochastic nature of the representation learned by variational autoencoders to augment the training data for our surrogate models. We utilize the regularized latent space of a variational point cloud autoencoder for fast design prototyping and data augmentation.
Design Phase: In automotive digital development, engineers utilize multiple virtual prototyping tools to design and assess the performance of 3D shapes. However, accurate performance simulations are computationally expensive and time-consuming, which may be prohibitive for design optimization tasks. To address this challenge, we envision a 3D design assistance system for design exploration with performance assessment in the automotive domain.
Design Practice/Task: we envision a 3D design assistance system for design exploration with performance assessment in the automotive domain. Recent advances in deep learning methods for learning geometric data are a promising step towards realizing such systems. by coupling the surrogate models to the autoencoders, we enable engineers to explore designs taking into account the estimated performance on different domains.

Article Title: 172_Beyond Manual Modeling Automating GUI Model Generation Using Design Documents
Design Discipline: Throughout the development lifecycle of a GUI application, various artifacts are produced, with UI/UX designs being particularly significant alongside source code, especially in the industrial production environments of large companies. UI/UX designs are crucial as they define the intended appearance and behavior of the application before implementation. Model-based testing (MBT) is an effective approach for this purpose. MBT uses abstract models to represent the expected behavior of the GUI, helping identify and address issues to maintain high-quality user experiences.
Data About: GUI models encapsulate the desired visual appearance and interactive behaviors of applications, facilitating various downstream tasks like model-based testing (MBT). Leveraging design documents, DemGen employs computer vision pre-trained models in conjunction with a rule-based correction mechanism to identify GUI elements and their intended behaviors as defined in those documents. Corporation S, a large global company whose applications are widely used, provided design documents for four commercial applications.
Data Modality: DemGen employs computer vision pre-trained models in conjunction with a rule-based correction mechanism to identify GUI elements and their intended behaviors as defined in those documents. Furthermore, in design documents, there are also texts that serve as expressions specifying transition types, conditions, etc. To capture this textual information, we utilize the OCR technique.
AI methods: SAM is a foundational CV model tailored for image segmentation tasks. Trained on massive datasets, it has achieved outstanding results in zero-shot image segmentation tasks without re-training or fine-tuning. In our implementation, we utilize the VGG model to extract the embeddings of the GUI screens. VGG is a deep-learning model and is demonstrated wide applicability in various visual tasks.
AI Assistance Types (Lee and Kim, 2025): In this paper, a novel approach named DemGen is proposed to seek a unique pathway for GUI model generation. Leveraging design documents, DemGen employs computer vision pre-trained models in conjunction with a rule-based correction mechanism to identify GUI elements and their intended behaviors as defined in those documents. Subsequently, the identified content is transformed into a formal GUI model adhering to the IFML modeling language.
Design Phase: In this paper, a novel approach named DemGen is proposed to seek a unique pathway for GUI model generation. Leveraging design documents, DemGen employs computer vision pre-trained models in conjunction with a rule-based correction mechanism to identify GUI elements and their intended behaviors as defined in those documents. We implemented DemGen into a tool and successfully integrate it into a real-world industrial environment.
Design Practice/Task: GUI models encapsulate the desired visual appearance and interactive behaviors of applications, facilitating various downstream tasks like model-based testing (MBT). Model-based testing (MBT) is an effective approach for this purpose. MBT uses abstract models to represent the expected behavior of the GUI, helping identify and address issues to maintain high-quality user experiences. Article Title: 136_The Generative Generic-Field Design Method Based on Design Cognition and Knowledge Reasoning
Design Discipline: In modern industry and smart manufacturing, industries are being reshaped by two forces: the power of crowds and artificial intelligence (AI) represented by large language models (LLMs). Under these two forces, the fields of engineering design and product innovation design are rapidly developing along two paths: crowd intelligence innovation (CII) represented by the Problem–Solution (P-S) Co-evolution Model, and AI-Generated Content (AIGC), which utilizes AI technology to generate content.
Data About: This study extracts the above design concepts from 39,463 patents to form the technical domain set T (|T|=582), the physical structural domain set S (|S|=4420), and the functional domain set F (|F|=24246). Chinese invention patents are selected as the knowledge source, and the relevant data can be downloaded from the Patent Data Service Pilot System of the State Intellectual Property Office.
Data Modality: Knowledge-based design (KBD) combined with design cognition (DC) mostly utilizes data sources in the form of natural language text. In this study, Chinese invention patents are selected as the knowledge source, and the relevant data can be downloaded from the Patent Data Service Pilot System of the State Intellectual Property Office. In this study, the full text of patent text is utilized to train the Skip-gram model so as to obtain the contextual semantic vectors of all the relevant knowledge elements.
AI methods: Based on the topic model, a statistical model widely used in semantic analysis and text mining, ConceptTM, is proposed to generate a set of physical structures containing implicit connections for a given technical field and functional requirements. ConceptTM adopts the PGM, especially the infrastructure of the topic model, to systematically express the complex and implicit correlations among physical structures. The modeling thinking of the probabilistic topic model is to regard the text as the probability distribution of the topic; that is, each text is composed of several topics, and the topic is regarded as the probability distribution of words.
AI Assistance Types (Lee and Kim, 2025): ConceptTM, a generative reasoning method, can perform Bayesian reasoning according to the given technical field and function to obtain the qualified physical set; G-Designer, a generative reasoning method, can mine their hierarchical relationships based on a given physical set to generate the design of the product architecture.
Design Phase: This study proposes a method based on design cognition and knowledge reasoning. Firstly, through the problem formulation, a generative universal domain design framework and knowledge base are constructed. Secondly, the knowledge-based discrete physical structure set generation method and system architecture generation method are proposed. Finally, the application tool Intelligent Design Assistant (IDA) is developed, verified, and discussed through an engineering design case.
Design Practice/Task: The product concept generation process into two phases: the first phase is responsible for discrete physical structure reasoning, and the second phase is responsible for physical structure relationship reasoning and interpretation to obtain a complete knowledge of the system architecture.

Article Title: 96_Product Innovation Design Method Based on BP Neural Network

AI methods: Bayesian optimization is a machine learning technique for performing optimization of black-box functions. AI methods: A deep neural network (DNN) is used as the ML model. Article Title: 189_The Application of Deep Learning for the Evaluation of User Interfaces
Design Discipline: In this study, we tested the ability of a machine-learning model (ML) to evaluate different user interface designs within the defined boundaries of some given software. Our approach used ML to automatically evaluate existing and new web application designs and provide developers and designers with a benchmark for choosing the most user-friendly and effective design.
Data About: This was accomplished by recording user accuracy and solution time as they used a web application that was developed for this research. The application records information about the user interface that was randomly assigned to the participant (layout, combination of colors, contrast, and type and size of the font), the question, the answers, the respondent's recorded answer, and the time the respondent spent answering. Response time was measured from the moment at which the interface of the specific question was fully loaded and displayed to the respondent until the moment at which the respondent answered the question and the application began loading the next question.
Data Modality: Based on the data collected on the appearance of the layout and the question asked, images of the user interface shown to the respondent were created and stored for deep learning purposes.
AI methods: To prove the hypothesis that CNNs can evaluate the effectiveness of a user interface, we tested a number of diverse architectures: vanilla CNNs, general-purpose networks modified for regression tasks such as VGG19 (Visual Geometry Group) [28], InceptionResNetV2 [29], Xception [30], and ResNet50 [31], and deep ensemble models for regression. CNN training was implemented with the Keras [32] and TensorFlow [33] deep-learning frameworks.
AI Assistance Types (Lee and Kim, 2025): The goal of this study was to test the hypothesis that a machine-learning model (ML) can evaluate the future performance of a user interface in terms of user response and that such a model is capable of evaluating different user interface designs within the defined boundaries of some given software. Our approach used ML to automatically evaluate existing and new web application designs and provide developers and designers with a benchmark for choosing the most user-friendly and effective design. The model can rank accessible designs and evaluate the accessibility of new designs.
Design Phase: We designed an ensemble model involving a multichannel custom CNN. CNN training was implemented with the Keras and TensorFlow deep-learning frameworks. Design Discipline: As an application for probabilistic reasoning, an example from engineering design is used to demonstrate a possible handling of uncertainty for the development of robust products. A rotary valve is to be used for the dosing of bulk food for hot drinks.
Data About: For the development of robust products, which are insensitive to uncertainties, it is important to assess possible effects of decisions made in the development process and to consider the relevant influencing factors of tolerances, environments and use cases
Data Modality: a sensor measures the required powder quantity, which is below its reference value with a probability of P(low) = 0.1
AI methods: In this paper a tool from the field of artificial intelligence, decision networks, is used. Decision networks utilize probabilistic reasoning to model uncertainties with probabilities. According to Russel and Norvig [17], the structure of a Bayesian network can be described by a directed acyclic graph (DAG) in which each node is annotated quantitative probability information.
AI Assistance Types (Lee and Kim, 2025): The method presented in this paper supports the engineer in decision-making by representing the uncertainties in a decision network using probabilities. With sensitivities, i.e. the minimum and maximum of a requirement corridor, possible use cases are determined with their probability of occurrence. Based on these, statements about the robustness of the product can be made.

Article Title: 98_Supervised deep learning for the moving morphable components topology optimization framework
Design Discipline: In the context of mechanical engineering design, the field of machine learning accelerated topology optimization is dominated by image-based models trained in a supervised manner on datasets with a limited diversity of boundary conditions.
Data About: Topology optimization (TO) is a numerical tool that aims to automatically solve the problem of efficiently distributing material inside a given domain to maximize specific mechanical properties. This structural optimization method, introduced by Bendsoe in 1988, proposes to represent the material distribution using a set of design variables, and then move toward an optimum by computing the objective function derivative with respect to the design variables.
Data Modality: Instead of using a sparse image as input to convey information on the BCs and desired volume fraction, we propose to use a dense description vector to convey the spatial attributes of the TO problem using coordinates.

Article Title: 158_A Novel User Emotional Interaction Design Model Using Long and Short-Term Memory Networks and Deep Learning
Design Discipline: Emotional design is an important development trend of interaction design. Emotional design in products plays a key role in enhancing user experience and inducing user emotional resonance. Good interaction design focuses on user experience. A good user experience is able to adapt to different types of users and the different emotions of each user.
Data About: Through the analysis of various characteristics, the speech is selected as the experimental material. Second, a speech-based emotion recognition method is proposed. The mel-Frequency cepstral coefficient (MFCC) of the speech signal is used as the input of the improved long and short-term memory network (ILSTM).
Data Modality: Through the analysis of various characteristics, the speech is selected as the experimental material. Second, a speech-based emotion recognition method is proposed. The mel-Frequency cepstral coefficient (MFCC) of the speech signal is used as the input of the improved long and short-term memory network (ILSTM).
AI methods: this research uses a deep learning mechanism for more accurate and effective emotion recognition, thereby optimizing the design of the interactive system and improving the user experience. Firstly, this research discusses how to use user characteristics such as speech, facial expression, video, heartbeat, etc., to make machines more accurately recognize human emotions. Through the analysis of various characteristics, the speech is selected as the experimental material. Second, a speech-based emotion recognition method is proposed. The mel-Frequency cepstral coefficient (MFCC) of the speech signal is used as the input of the improved long and short-term memory network (ILSTM).
AI Assistance Types (Lee and Kim, 2025): In the emotional interaction design, the machine needs to capture the user's key information in real time, recognize the user's emotional state, and use a variety of clues to finally determine the appropriate user model. The emotion model module needs to study various psychological phenomena of the user, and make a reasonable response to the user's emotions by judging the composition of the emotional information.
Design Phase: Second, a speech-based emotion recognition method is proposed. The mel-Frequency cepstral coefficient (MFCC) of the speech signal is used as the input of the improved long and short-term memory network (ILSTM). The emotional features obtained by ILSTM are input into the attention layer, and the self-attention mechanism is used to calculate the weight of each frame of speech signal. Experiments on the EMO-DB and CASIA datasets verify the effectiveness of the model for emotion recognition.
Design Practice/Task: Based on the emotion recognition function, an emotional interaction design plan is proposed. The ultimate goal of this research is to enhance the user's product experience and truly put people first.

Article Title: 125_Machine learning-based design support system for the prediction of heterogeneous machine parameters in industry 4.0

This paper aims to fill this gap by introducing an innovative Design Support System (DesSS), originated from the Decision Support System, for the prediction and estimation of machine specification data such as machine geometry and machine design on the basis of heterogeneous input parameters.
Data About: This paper aims to fill this gap by introducing an innovative Design Support System (DesSS), originated from the Decision Support System, for the prediction and estimation of machine specification data such as machine geometry and machine design on the basis of heterogeneous input parameters.

AI methods: As the main core of the developed DesSS, we introduced different machine learning (ML) approaches based on Decision/Regression Tree, k-Nearest Neighbors, and Neighborhood Component Features Selection.

Design Phase: This paper aims to fill this gap by introducing an innovative Design Support System (DesSS), originated from the Decision Support System, for the prediction and estimation of machine specification data such as machine geometry and machine design on the basis of heterogeneous input parameters. Design Practice/Task: The main goal of this work was to create a system that brings together in an interesting tool many years of experience of designers and laboratory technicians. For instance, the algorithm is capable of predicting the machine geometry and material on the basis of several input parameters: machine performance (torque, efficiency, speed, etc.), final application, market, and cost range.
